characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Rizzo's on-again-off-again girlfriend, introduced as one of the rat crowd inThe Muppets Take Manhattanand appearing sporadically in Muppet productions ever since. Ascended Extra: InThe Muppets Take Manhattan, she wasThe Smurfetteof Rizzo's group of rats and though she's definitely the non-Rizzo rat to make the most appearances in productions afterwards, she didn't appear very often and usually only for cameos. Inthe 2015 show, she became a regular character as Kermit's secretary. Deadpan Snarker: She can more than keep up with Rizzo. Hidden Depths: Apparently, she's studying to become an ultrasound technician (she has a B average). Jerk with a Heart of Gold: Extremely sarcastic, sometimes all too willing to step on other people's toes and not always the most moral of rats — but not a bad person.

characters.name: Yolanda Rat
characters.description: Rizzo's on-again-off-again girlfriend, introduced as one of the rat crowd inThe Muppets Take Manhattanand appearing sporadically in Muppet productions ever since. Ascended Extra: InThe Muppets Take Manhattan, she wasThe Smurfetteof Rizzo's group of rats and though she's definitely the non-Rizzo rat to make the most appearances in productions afterwards, she didn't appear very often and usually only for cameos. Inthe 2015 show, she became a regular character as Kermit's secretary. Deadpan Snarker: She can more than keep up with Rizzo. Hidden Depths: Apparently, she's studying to become an ultrasound technician (she has a B average). Jerk with a Heart of Gold: Extremely sarcastic, sometimes all too willing to step on other people's toes and not always the most moral of rats — but not a bad person.

characters.name: Bean Bunny
characters.description: Created for a one-offHBOspecial, Bean Bunny went on to become a recurring character inThe Jim Henson Hourand late-periodMuppet Babies, and had a major role inMuppet*Vision 3D. He's childlike and cute, he knows it...and he uses it to curry sympathy when his attempts to help the show go awry. The Bus Came Back: Bean made two surprise appearances via social media at the end of 2025. He appeared in a Disney Vacation Club Christmas video with Miss Piggy, and with Fozzie Bear on The Muppets social media for a New Year's Eve greeting. He is now being performed by Bradley Freeman Jr., who also now performs Rizzo. Butt-Monkey: In most Muppet productions, when he appears, it's to get clobbered or otherwise go through some sort of physical abuse. Usually this is played for laughs, but inThe Muppet Christmas Carol, one scene turns him into a deeply tragic character, when after having gone through his usual comical clobberings (delivered by Scrooge), he isrevealed to be homeless and shivering in the cold winter night. Characterization Marches On: InThe Tale of the Bunny Picnic, he's a classicKid Herowith awild imagination, who struggles withnot being taken seriouslybecause he's so little, but whose resourcefulness and courage save the day in the end. Once he joined the regular Muppet cast, his personality was retooled to make him "so cute he's annoying" and aButt-Monkey. Demoted to Extra: Likely due to Steve Whitmire taking over the role of Kermit after Jim Henson's passing, Bean Bunny has been relegated to being a background character since the mid-'90s.

characters.name: The Elvises
characters.description: Elvish Presley: In their debut, they were the stars of a rather unconventional telling ofThe Elves and the Shoemaker, thanks to Rizzo initially mishearing the title. Elvis Impersonator: The Elvises have had many performers over the years, all of whom count as these. Part of the fun is hearing the subtle differences between the different performers' imitations. Elvis Lives: And there are three of him!Nobody finds this odd. Put on a Bus: AfterMuppets Tonightwas cancelled. Spotlight-Stealing Squad: They don't really interact with the main cast much. They appear, rock out, and leave.

characters.name: Andy and Randy Pig
characters.description: Catchphrase: They tended to always say "This job's too hard!" when assigned to almost any task onMuppet's Tonight. Demoted to Extra: AfterMuppets Tonightwas cancelled, they've largely been demoted to background characters in such works asThe Muppets (2015)andMuppets Haunted Mansion.

characters.name: The Horrible Goose
characters.description: When Beaker demos Dr. Honeydew's new VR headset at the Game Awards something goes horribly wrong, bringing the main character ofUntitled Goose Gameinto the real world. Crossover: The main character of Indie Game Sleeper hitUntitled Goose Gamejoins the Muppets to torment Beaker. Feathered Fiend: Somehow escapes from its game to continue tormenting Beaker and steal the winner' envelope.

characters.name: Clifford
characters.description: Part of theMuppeTelevisioncrew at his debut, Clifford went on to be the host/straight man ofMuppets Tonightin 1996. Ascended Extra: He actually started off as this, as a new member of the Electric Mayhem in some later specials (includingThe Muppets at Walt Disney World) and then got promoted to a main character forMuppets Tonight. Now he's come full-circle... Cool Shades: He couldn't wear them onMuppets Tonight, much to Kevin Clash's chagrin. Deadpan Snarker: It's very rare, but he is known for having a deadpan sense of humor on occasion. Demoted to Extra: He was one of the last Muppets designed by Jim Henson, and was given a big push throughout the late-'80s to mid-'90s. However, he all but disappeared afterMuppets Tonightwent off the air, and his character has seemingly been retired.

characters.name: Digit
characters.description: Early-Bird Cameo: Was in Jim Henson's original pilot ofThe Jim Henson Hour, titledInner Tube TV. Digit was the keyboard player for the all-new band and had a more-robotic voice. Digit is also the only Muppet from said pilot to remain on board when the show was changed toThe Jim Henson Hour. Put on a Bus: Largely vanished afterThe Jim Henson Hourwas cancelled; he would only appear again two decades later in the video gameMuppets Party Cruise. Robot Buddy: He appears to be robotic, and serves as something of Kermit's sidekick duringMuppeTelevision.

characters.name: Lindbergh
characters.description: Percussive Maintenance: He often tries to fix things simply by smashing them with a sledgehammer, only to break them worse. Put on a Bus: He was effectively dropped as a character afterThe Jim Henson Hourwas cancelled, making only brief appearances in the short story collectionMuppets Meet the Classics: Fairy Tales from the Brothers Grimm.

characters.name: Vicki
characters.description: Innocently Insensitive: The episode "First Show" has her accidentally irritating Kermit by reminding him of how old he is compared to her, Vicki having been in daycare whenThe Muppet Showwas running. Put on a Bus: She hasn't appeared sinceThe Jim Henson Hourwas cancelled.

characters.name: Leon
characters.description: Ambiguously Related: He calls Kermit "cuz", despite their being different species. Put on a Bus: He hasn't appeared sinceThe Jim Henson Hourwas cancelled.

characters.name: Flash
characters.description: Put on a Bus: Hasn't been seen sinceThe Jim Henson Hourwas cancelled, aside from a few cameos inMuppets Tonight. The Unintelligible: Mumbles a lot, making him very hard to understand most of the time. He does sing clearly though.

characters.name: Beard
characters.description: Messy Hair: Has a very unkempt mop of hair on his head and face. Put on a Bus: Aside from a brief appearance in the 2010 comic bookMuppet Snow White, he hasn't appeared sinceThe Jim Henson Hourwas cancelled.

characters.name: Solid Foam Drummer
characters.description: No Name Given: She's never identified by name. Put on a Bus: Only appeared in three episodes and hasn't been seen since. The Quiet One: Only has a single line in the series — "Drum machine.", revealing she isn't even the one playing her own instrument.

characters.name: Zondra/Darci
characters.description: Demoted to Extra: AfterThe Jim Henson Hourwas cancelled, she had a few appearances onMuppets Tonightunder the name Darci, but has only made silent cameos since. Goth: Has the appearance of one as both Zondra and Darci, with pale skin, black lipstick and a black leather outfit; in the latter identity, she's specifically described as an angry goth rocker-type.

characters.name: Ubu
characters.description: Put on a Bus: Only appeared twice onThe Jim Henson Hourand hasn't been seen since.

characters.name: Waldo C. Graphic
characters.description: "The spirit of 3D", he features heavily inMuppet*Vision 3D. Fad Super:Muppet*Vision 3Dwas his finest hour (and it played at Disney theme parks for well over three decades), but his VR manipulation and outdated CGI gave Waldo a short shelf life indeed. You'd think with 3-D movies being all the rage in the late 2000s-early 2010s, he would have made a return, but alas... Meaningful Name: He's a computergraphiccontrolled by a puppeteer wearingwaldos. Mix-and-Match Critters: He looks like a cross between a butterfly and a seal. Non-Standard Character Design: In contrast to the other Muppets, which are all traditional puppets, Waldo is a uniquely CGI puppet controlled digitally by the muppeteer.

characters.name: Chip
characters.description: A nerdy human Muppet who was initially rather unknown, but made a comeback as an IT guy onUp Late With Miss Piggy. Ascended Extra: Managed to go from being an obscure Muppet on a short-running show to being a recurring character inthe 2015 show. Author Avatar: He's a caricature of Bill Prady. The fact that Prady was a producer for the 2015 show is the reason he managed to come back. Catchphrase: "I'm Chip. I'm the IT guy." The Friend Nobody Likes: In the 2015 show, he's treated as this due to his obscurity and weirdness, having been pranked at least once via email. He doesn't mind.

characters.name: Pepe the King Prawn
characters.description: A Spanish prawn with an odd speech pattern. He often attempts to be a womanizer. Accidental Misnaming: He's terrible at getting people's names right, even when he's looking at a card with their name on it. Ambiguously Bi: Usually seen flirting with ladies, Muppet or human, but he also admits he findsJosh Grobanattractive.Pepe:What? He's a handsome man, and gender is fluid!There's aGenius Bonushere: For prawns, and a few species of shrimp, genderisfluid; they can be born male and become female later in life.invoked There's aGenius Bonushere: For prawns, and a few species of shrimp, genderisfluid; they can be born male and become female later in life.invoked Ascended Extra: Became an ascended extra starting withMuppets From Space, but came full circle when he wasDemoted to Extrafor the 2011 movie. He got re-promoted forthe 2015 show.

characters.name: Bobo the Bear
characters.description: Ascended Extra: TheonlyMuppets TonightMuppet to appear for more than one scene or crowd shot in the 2011 film. Beary Friendly: Much like Fozzie, even offering a stressed-out Kermit a hug in one episode of the 2015 series. Breakout Character: One of the very few post-1990 Muppet characters to become a full-fledged member of the core cast. Dad the Veteran: In the 2015 series he has a daughter in the girl scouts and a past spent fixing helicopters for "The Service". Such skills arenottransferable to the fixing of elevators. Demoted to Extra: Along with Uncle Deadly inMuppets Most Wanted. They go from fairly major characters in the previous film to just brief cameos near the end.

characters.name: Johnny Fiama
characters.description: Demoted to Extra: His appearances became much more limited after 2005, with theBohemian Rhapsodyvideo from 2009 being his final appearance to date; his performer Bill Barretta confirmed in 2015 that his puppet had deteriorated (along with Sal Minella's). The two finally reappeared in 2021'sMuppets Haunted Mansionas ghosts. Heterosexual Life-Partners: With Sal Minella. Meaningful Name: Fiama is an anagram of Mafia, a reference to the rumor that Frank Sinatra achieved fame through his underworld connections. Momma's Boy: Johnny Fiama loves his mama. UST: WithMiss Piggy, of all people.

characters.name: Sal Minella
characters.description: Demoted to Extra: Just like with Johnny Fiama, his appearances became much more limited after 2005, mostly because his performer Brian Henson stopped being a full-time performer; theBohemian Rhapsodyvideo from 2009 was his final appearance to date for a long time, Bill Barretta (Johnny Fiama's performer) confirming in 2015 that his puppet had deteriorated (along with Johnny's). The two finally returned as ghosts in the 2021 Disney+ specialMuppets Haunted Mansion, with Brian Henson returning to perform Sal. Early-Bird Cameo: He makes a (very) brief appearance during the opening number ofMuppets Treasure Island. Heterosexual Life-Partners: With Johnny Fiama. Those Two Guys: He and Johnny are practically inseparable.

characters.name: Seymour
characters.description: Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: Hey everybody, remember when Pepe had an elephant sidekick? Dogged Nice Guy: Compared to Pepe, it was no question who the more interesting one was. Honorable Elephant: He’s certainly a nice guy all things considered. Put on a Bus: After Muppets Tonight was cancelled.

characters.name: Mr. Poodlepants
characters.description: Demoted to Extra: He's only made cameos sinceMuppets Tonightwas cancelled. No Celebrities Were Harmed: His fluttery voice and clownish persona were based on that of comedianEd Wynn.

characters.name: Nigel
characters.description: Ambiguous Species: He's a green... thing, with a long snout. Mood-Swinger: His mood shifts from mellow to frantic or vice versa, depending on how well the show is going and how much coffee he's had.

characters.name: Spamela Hamderson
characters.description: Brainless Beauty: Neither she nor the character she plays are all that bright. Head-Turning Beauty: Champ Schwimmer falls intoGibberish of Lovewhen he first sees her. Lifeguard Fanservice: The very purpose of her existence is to parody this trope. Her puppet is designed to be noticeablybusty(not to mention is wearing a bikini), and is so attractive that her fellow lifeguard Champ Schwimmerconstantly ignores what's happening on the beachin favor of making out with her. Put on a Bus: Has only made brief cameos sinceMuppets Tonightwas cancelled.

characters.name: Dr. Phil Van Neuter
characters.description: The Bus Came Back: Van Neuter returned for the first time in two decades inMuppets Haunted Mansion. Freudian Excuse: This guy's been messed up his whole life. Nepotism: He hired Mulch because Mulch is the brother of Phil's wife, Composta Heap. Put on a Bus: Aside from a brief role inMuppets from Spaceand a cameo inMuppets Haunted Mansion, he hasn't been seen sinceMuppets Tonight.

characters.name: Mulch
characters.description: Early-Bird Cameo: He originally appeared onThe Muppet Showduring its fifth season and as aFrankenstein's Monsterin theNephew of Frankensteinsketch fromThe Muppets Go to the Movies. The Igor: He's a grotesque brute who serves as Dr. Phil Van Neuter's assistant. The Unintelligible: Became this onMuppets Tonight. With the exception of the end credits' blooper reels, he does not say anything other than inarticulate growls. Put on a Bus: AfterMuppets Tonightwas cancelled. Nearly two decades later, however, he returned in a cameo inMuppets Haunted Mansion.

characters.name: Big Mean Carl
characters.description: Accessory-Wearing Cartoon Animal: Sometimes, he wears nothing at all, but at other times, he just wears a necktie or a set of bunny ears. The Bad Guy Wins: Because every contestant who appears on Swift Wits is so unbelievably stupid that they always fail, the segment always ends with him eating the animal whose life was put on the line. Taken to an extreme in the one segment where the contestant wins, where hestilleats the animal anyway and then eats the contestant. The closest he gets to any punishment is in the third segment where one of the props falls on his hands, which causes him to shout in pain. Big Eater: He continues the proud tradition of Muppet monsters with voracious appetites. The Big Guy: He's one of the bigger Muppets. It says so in his name. Breakout Character: Like Pepe the King Prawn and Bobo the Bear, he is one of the very few post-1990 Muppets who is still a prominent recurring character in Muppet productions, albeit not as prominently as Pepe or Bobo.

characters.name: Gonzo's Family
characters.description: Act of True Love:When Gonzo decides he wants to stay on earth with his friends, his family isn't at all angered and support him staying. Their leader says they will miss him, but they respect and understand his decision. Long-Lost Relative: Gonzo's long last relatives to be precise. Their exact relations to him are never specified, though it is implied their leader, the Ubergonzo, is Gonzo's older brother. Nice Guy: They are all incredibly jovial and friendly.They don't even care that Ed tried to kill them, when he makes them laugh they take him in as an ambassador. Searching for the Lost Relative: It is never fully explained, but somehow Gonzo got separated from them and they have been trying to find him ever since. Strange Minds Think Alike: Just like Gonzo, they love firing themselves out of cannons.

characters.name: Walter
characters.description: Ascended Fanboy: The lucky guy gets to meet his idols, help them come back to relevance, and even gets a part in their telethon and becomes a member of the gang! Audience Surrogate: As Tough Pigs put it, "(...)Walter is us. I related with his character so much, I practically expected him to say that he runs his own website for Muppet Fans Who Grew Up." Author Appeal:Jason Segel, who co-wrote the 2011Muppetsfilm (and also portrays Gary) grew up watching the originalMuppet Showand has a great affection for the characters, and Walter's own love of the Muppets was heavily influenced by this. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: He's completely absent fromthe 2015 showaside from the 10-minute presentation pilot, which is very notable considering that far less important Muppets made appearances in it and his puppeteer worked on the show. He ultimately returned in 2017, but then he was absent fromThe Muppet Showspecial. Demoted to Extra: InMuppets Most Wanted. Until he and Fozzie discover the truth about Constantine at the beginning of the final act, he's barely in the movie, with only a few lines here and there.

characters.name: '80s Robot
characters.description: Artificial Intelligence: Well, heisa robot, so this is a given. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: Unlike Walter, who was only absent temporarily afterMuppets Most Wanted, '80s Robot's absence is another story as '80s Robot has yet to make appearances outside ofMuppets Most Wanted. Computer Voice: He speaks in a stereotypically monotonous robotic voice. Demoted to Extra: InMuppets Most Wanted, where he doesn't have any lines at all. Disco Dan: Apparently never got the input code that TAB and New Coke are out of vogue.

characters.name: The Moopets
characters.description: A thug styled Muppets tribute band from Reno,Nevada. They were originally headlined by the real Fozzie Bear, but aside from him group shares very few similarities with who they are based on. Ascended Extra: Miss Poogy would end up joining the actual Muppets towards the end of the movie. Originally she was a temporary replacement for Miss Piggy, but she continued to show up in Muppets shows and movies afterwards, though mostly in minor roles. Badbutt: Even though they don't appear swearing, drinking or taking drugs, unlike many real-life humans in similar positions, the Moopets are considerably ruder, uglier, and trashier than the Muppets. Bears Are Bad News: Foozie is a bear who serves as Fozzie'sEvil Counterpart, and in fact replaced Fozzie when Fozzie left the Moopets to reunite with the Muppets. Darker and Edgier: They're all dark and gritty thugs styled after the Muppets.

characters.name: Constantine
characters.description: The world's most dangerous frog. He just so happens to look exactly like Kermit the Frog, save for a mole on his face. Accessory-Wearing Cartoon Animal: Like Kermit, his only article of clothing is a green collar, though this one is shorter than Kermit's. Amphibian Assault: He's a frog who serves as Kermit'sEvil Counterpart. He breaks out of prison, frames Kermit to get him arrested, and takes over the Muppets in an attempt to steal priceless jewels. Big Bad: He's theWorld's Most Dangerous Frog. Having recently escaped from Russia's most fortified prison, he's out to make his name as the greatest thief in history by stealing England's Crown Jewels, and manipulating the Muppets to realize that goal. Card-Carrying Villain: He relishes in his criminal status, and wants to steal the famous Crown Jewels of England to ensure that his name will go down in history as "Greatest Thief of All Time".

characters.name: Denise
characters.description: Kermit's girlfriend in the 2015 show. They got together shortly after Kermit and Piggy had their big break-up. Age-Gap Romance: Downplayed to a degree, but the way Miss Piggy talks about her, she's significantly younger than Kermit. Early Installment Character-Design Difference: In the show's pilot, she is portrayed by the Spamela Hamderson puppet, before getting her own unique design in the show proper. Romantic False Lead: Given that she's in the way of one of TV's most famous couples, this is inevitable,especially after she and Kermit split in "Swine Song". Southern-Fried Genius: Born in Georgia, and speaks with a prominent southern accent, she's pretty leveled headed, and at one point tried (but ultimately failed) to talkKermitout of making a reckless decision.

characters.name: Gloria Estefan
characters.description: An adorable baby penguin that Piggy takes home with her from a trip to Argentina. No relation tothe singer. Ambiguous Gender: Piggy calls them a "he", while Deadly calls them a "she". Misplaced Wildlife: Gloria is clearly an Emperor Penguin... and from Argentina, there are no Emperor Penguins in Argentina Named After Somebody Famous: Only because Piggy couldn't think of any other Spanish-sounding names. Non-Standard Character Design: Looks more like a stuffed animal than the average Muppet.

characters.name: Joe the Legal Weasel
characters.description: A weasel who serves as the Muppets' legal consultant as well as providing legal disclaimers before shorts. His job typically causes problems for Kermit and Scooter as they try to run the show, as well as for Dr. Bunsen Honeydew and Beaker for obvious reasons. Cannot Tell a Joke: His attempts at humor are probably even worse than Fozzie's. Do Not Try This at Home: He provides this warning before every Muppet Labs Field Test short onNow. He also makes sure that the warning isn't just limited to home. Early-Bird Cameo: He appeared almost a full year before the release ofMuppets Now, both to announce the show, and for the Muppets' Talk Like A Pirate Day video (in which he talks like someone committing digital piracy). Evil Lawyer Joke: He doesn't hesitate to joke about his species and profession.

characters.name: Beverly Plume
characters.description: A turkey who hosts the Økėÿ Døkęÿ Køøkïñ shorts. She tries to explain the dishes being prepared and introducing the celebrity guests while simultaneously dealing with the Swedish Chef's antics. Not So Above It All: She's not as nuts as the Swedish Chef, but she's still a Muppet. On a few occasions, she gets distracted flirting with any male contestants on the cooking challenge. Straight Man: To the Swedish Chef. Verbal Tic: She's prone to making turkey noises whenever she gets excited or flustered.

characters.name: Penny Waxman
characters.description: The CEO of Waxtown Records, who gave the Electric Mayhem an advance to record an album decades ago. She has a romantic history with Dr. Teeth and a rather crotchety personality. Ambiguously Jewish: Frequently uses Yiddish terms and speaks in this sort of accent. Big Eater: She gorges on hot wings and spicy chips. Brutal Honesty: She says exactly what's on her mind, which she credits with getting her where she is today. Fan Disservice: Designed to resemble an overweight old lady, and frequent focus is put on her feet.

characters.name: Tina Teeth and Gerald Teeth, Sr.
characters.description: Dr. Teeth's parents, both dentists who show up every decade or so to try and bring their son back into the family business. Beehive Hairdo: Tina wears her hair this way, having it teased very high above her head. Determinator: They won't give up on getting their boy to live up to his promise of becoming a dentist. Fantasy-Forbidding Father: Tina is aghast at her son evenassociatingwith a rock-n-roller like Floyd; let alonebecomingone himself. Henpecked Husband: On the surface, Tina is a domineering force who drags her husband around to be herYes-Man, and she's clearly the one in charge of their practice. However, it's subverted in that he's absolutely relaxed and content with the setup, and when he actually does disagree with her, he lets it be known. She also isn't as cold as most examples, praising him when they get the Golden Tooth award.

characters.name: Doc Hopper
characters.description: Proprietor of a French-fried frog legs fast food franchise, Doc Hopper sees Kermit the Frog as the ideal spokesman for his product. Kermit refuses to shill for him, but Hopperreallywon't take no for an answer... Big Bad: He is the main villain ofThe Muppet Movie. Fat, Sweaty Southerner in a White Suit: He's fat, wears a white suit, sells fried food, and is evil. Hidden Depths: While he quickly shakes it off, he's visibly hurt when Max reveals he's not his friend. If I Can't Have You…: Doc decides that if he can't have Kermit as a spokesperson, he will have him killed. Kermit even tells him in the climax that if he really isn't redeemable at heart, he might as well go ahead and kill him... a challenge Doc Hopper accepts.

characters.name: Max
characters.description: Doc Hopper's somewhat nerdy assistant brought Kermit to his boss's attention after seeing the frog not only ride a bicycle, but also perform with Fozzie Bear at El Sleezo's Cafe. He's initially supportive of Hopper's attempts to make Kermit a spokesfrog, but soon enough he's only sticking with the bad guy for the money, and even that motivation only goes so far... Heel–Face Turn: He never actually joins the good guys, but he warns Kermit and the gang about the frog killer, tells Doc he isn’t his friend anymore, and is overjoyed whenthe giant Animalscares Doc Hopper away. Minion with an F in Evil: He's not very good at being an evil henchman.

characters.name: Nicky Holiday
characters.description: Amazon Brigade: His gang consists of three girls named Carla, Darla, and Marla. Bait the Dog: Nicky's only virtue is that his crush on Miss Piggy is sincere and he likes her spirit. Then he frames her for theft so that he won't get caught. Unsurprisingly, Miss Piggy turns him down at the end of the movie before the cops take him away and says that while she accepts his apology, she'd rather be with Kermit. Contrasting Sequel Antagonist: Nicky serves as this to Doc Hopper fromThe Muppet Movie. Doc is a restaurateur who is more associated with Kermit, while Nicky is the brother of Lady Holiday and is more associated with Miss Piggy. Doc wanted Kermit to be the mascot for his french-fried frog legs restaurant, while Nicky wanted to frame Miss Piggy for jewelry theft so he could have her arrested while he stole the baseball diamond. Doc chased Kermit halfway across the country, while Nicky stayed in London the entire time. Doc had a whole army of men working for him, including aMad Scientistand an assassin, whereas Nicky only had three right-hand women. Finally, while Docran away after being scared off by a giant Animal, Nicky was defeated by Miss Piggy and ended up arrested. Crazy-Prepared: Nicky and his supermodel cat burglars have all the highest tech equipment, which is put in contrast to the Muppets having nonsensical items to counter with (like a Whoopee cushion), but even then, they forgot or lost them. In one case, Animal ate one of the items. Crouching Moron, Hidden Badass: Nicky seems like a complete moron... until you learn he's the film'sBig Bad.

characters.name: Ebenezer Scrooge
characters.description: The main protagonist ofA Christmas Caroland a greedy old grump who hates Christmas. Adaptational Intelligence: A minor example, but in the future visit he's one of the few Scrooges that figures out the fates of both himself and Tiny Tim before either is explicitly spelled out to him. Adaptational Jerkass: Whereas in the book, Scrooge is merely a passive-aggressive grump about Christmas and a genuinelyHonest Corporate Executivedespite his heartlessness, here he threatens to fire his writing staff just for asking about the cold and throws a wreath at Bean Bunny for caroling. Adaptational Personality Change: That said, in the book, he's amused by his nephew making jokes about him at Christmas. Here, he's actuallyhurt. Anti-Hero: The same tropes from the original book can fit him here.

characters.name: Long John Silver
characters.description: TheBig Badof the film (of course) and a fierce pirate captain who is out to find the treasure onTreasure Island. Adaptational Nice Guy: Unlike in the book, he uses nonlethal means to get rid of Mr. Arrow and later his mutinous crew, and he genuinely tries to keep Jim safe even when trying to steal Flint's treasure for himself, while in the book, he was willing to threaten Jim despite his fondness for the boy. Affably Evil: He's usually nice to Jim. Alas, Poor Villain: The scene in which he prepares to leave in a rowboat at the crack of dawn and that scene being the last time he'll ever see Jim is sad. It's made even more sad when Long John decides he would rather not shoot Jim where he stands. He even tears up when Jim rejects him. Anti-Villain: As far as he's concerned, he and his men are just reclaiming property that's rightfully theirs; it's Smollet and Trelawney who are the thieves swooping in to take the treasure just because some kid handed them the map. (Of course, the treasure is plunder in the first place, but....)

characters.name: K. Edgar Singer
characters.description: The main antagonist ofMuppets From Spaceand leader of the government agencyC.O.V.N.E.T, who seeks to capture Gonzo. Anti-Villain: He's takes pretty dark measures, but that's because he believes Gonzo is the scout for an alien invasion. Beleaguered Boss: To his assistant Rentro (Played by Bobo the Bear). Rentro's antics leave him exasperated. Berserk Button: Being laughed at. Even if it's just playful, being laughed at causes him to goSuddenly Shouting. Freudian Excuse: HisMotive Rantafter the first time hisBerserk Buttonis pushed makes it clear that he's been the butt of the joke for alongtime.

characters.name: Gary
characters.description: The main human protagonist of the 2011 film who sets out alongside his Muppet brother Walter (don't ask) to reunite the Muppets. Big Brother Mentor: To Walter. Hidden Depths: He and Walter are given the task of putting costumes together, and he briefly mentions that he and Walter are both "really good sewers". The Lancer: To Walter. He has Walter's back, though also brings up important questions regarding Walter's plans. Nice Guy: Very kind and supportive of his brother, Walter.

characters.name: Mary
characters.description: A schoolyard teacher and theLove Interestto Gary. She comes with Walter and Gary on their quest to reunite the Muppets. Cool Teacher: Her students even simultaneously admit they'll miss her during their two-week Spring Break. Deus ex Machina:When the Muppet Theater loses power during the telethon, she abruptly appears to repair the fuse box.Chekhov's Gunman: Her knowing how to fix the fuse box was foreshadowed early on, when she's teaching kids how to repair cars. It's her sudden appearance when she was in another state that makes itDeus ex Machina. Chekhov's Gunman: Her knowing how to fix the fuse box was foreshadowed early on, when she's teaching kids how to repair cars. It's her sudden appearance when she was in another state that makes itDeus ex Machina. Foil: Arguably to Miss Piggy — then again, "Me Party" shows that the two aren't so different.

characters.name: Tex Richman
characters.description: The prospect of oil under the site of the oldMuppet Showtheater prompts this billionaire to buy the building under the guise of turning it into a museum. When the good guys learn of his scheme to raze it, their quest to raise enough money to buy it back begins. He's got all the trappings of a classic family movie villain... except theEvil Laugh. Ax-Crazy: Not in the sense that he wants to kill anyone, but near the end of the movie he becomes hellbent on destroying everything in his path to ruin the Muppets' career. The Bad Guy Wins:Technically, since the Muppetsdofail to meet their goal of raising ten million dollars. In the end, it doesn't matter, due to his bowling ball-inducedHeel–Face Turn. Big Bad: Of the 2011 film. Contrasting Sequel Antagonist: To Doc Hopper. Hopper uses illegal methods in his pursuit of the Muppets, whereas Richman exploits legal loopholes to outplay the group. Hopperliterallywants the Muppets dead, whereas Richman onlymetaphoricallywants them gone. Hopper’s goons are all humans, while Richman’s affiliates are all former Muppets and Muppet impersonators. Finally, while Hopper proves to beBeyond Redemption, Richman is able to pull aHeel–Face Turncourtesy of Gonzo and Fozzie.

characters.name: Dominic Badguy
characters.description: Constantine's right-hand man, Dominic gets tangled up with the Muppets even before his boss does, suggesting that they go on a world tour and kicking off the movie's plot. Affably Evil: He's surprisingly polite for the right-hand man of an international thief. Beard of Evil: Ricky Gervais keeps his beard for the role as main antagonist Constantine's second in command. Butt-Monkey: He's treated like this in the song "I'm Number One", with a door swinging into his face being one such example of him being a victim of this trope. Evil Brit: For a given measure of "evil", given how he's rather affable. Still, hedoeswork for and attempt to overthrow the film's villain.

characters.name: Nadya
characters.description: A feisty Russian prison guard who loves Broadway and has a crush on Kermit. Ascended Fangirl: In an unconventional sense. She has a crush on Kermit and gets to keep him as a prisoner in her gulag, listen to a performance from him, and even joins in on the final musical number of the movie. Clingy Jealous Girl:It turns out that Nadya knew all along that Kermit is not Constantine, but is keeping him detained out of her intense fangirl crush on the A-list amphibian. Dreadful Musician: She's... enthusiastic, at least, but her singing of "I Need This Job" alarms Kermit. Whether she actually is or isn't, we don't get to see, because just as she gets to her big solo number...the movie ends. Genre Savvy: Works out most of Kermit's escape plans owing to her watching many prison films on Netflix.Breaking out with the cover of a show wasn't something she saw coming.

characters.name: Jean Pierre Napoleon
characters.description: A French Interpol who teams up with Sam Eagle (against each others' will) to find stolen paintings. Arch-Enemy: He had dedicated his career to finding his most elusive foe, the Lemur. Casanova Wannabe: He tries to do "Good cop, Romantic cop" with Ms. Piggy, but his flirting with her focusing on pig puns gets her to lash out at him. Foil: He is a European counterpart to Sam Eagle. Everything that is exaggerated about Sam's American items is just as exaggerated for him (such as a comically small mug to Sam's comically large mug). Interpol Special Agent: Although he's French, his job has him moving across the whole continent.

characters.name: Becky
characters.description: Fozzie's human girlfriend, who sometimes gets involved in the antics aroundUp Late With Miss Piggy. Dating What Daddy Hates: Her dad is shown to have issues with her dating a bear. Her mom tends more towardsInnocently Insensitive. Interspecies Romance: With Fozzie.

characters.name: Pache
characters.description: A branding guru that Royce hires to help runUp Late With Miss Piggyalongside Kermit. However, he tends to have bad ideas that make him little more than a nuisance around the office. Big Bad: Of the second half of the season. Freudian Excuse: He has a strained relationship with his distant, and suggested to be emotionally cold, father. Yolanda, Rizzo and Pepe manage to open communications between them, helping him become a nicer person. Hate Sink: You’re not meant to like this guy, as he is portrayed as an obnoxious executive who causes problems for the Muppets. That being said, he does get a pretty heartwarming send off all things considered. Manipulative Bastard: Is not above using underhanded tactics to get his way, from having guest stars pulled at the last minute to bribing the writers with new suits.

characters.name: Nora Singh
characters.description: An assistant at the failing Wax Town records, who tries to help the Electric Mayhem record their first album. All Work vs. All Play: The "All Work" to the Mayhem's "All Play". Defrosting Ice Queen: She initially works with Electric Mayhem so she can prove herself at her job and is annoyed by their quirks. As early as the first episode, she begins to warm up to them. Foil: To the entire band. They aredisorganized, spacey eccentrics who can't ever stay focusedbut whoare in-universe musical geniuses; while she's very orderly and pragmatic; but despite loving rock n' roll doesn't fully "get" it. Insufferable Imbecile: Not normally, but when it comes to rock n' roll; she's confident that the lyrics to theKISSsong are "I wanna rock n roll all night/Andpart ofevery day", no matter how many times she's corrected.

characters.name: Gary "Moog" Moogowski
characters.description: The Electric Mayhem's biggest fan, who follows them on-tour to every concert and aspires to be a musician himself. Endearingly Dorky: As Hannah puts it to Nora, "It seems like Awkward Guy is growing on you". She also describes his crush on her sister as "adorable". Meaningful Name: Moog, an aspiring music producer, shares his nickname with theMoog synthesizer, the first commerically-available music synthesizer. Only Sane Man: Only he and Zoot don't get high onexpired marshmallowsin the desert. Zoot beingZoot, he ends up having to act as the grounded trip-sitter. Opposites Attract: He's laid-back and a little awkward; and has a crush on straightlaced businesswoman Nora, which she eventually returns.

characters.name: Hannah Singh
characters.description: Nora's little sister, and a successful influencer. Rich Sibling, Poor Sibling: She's the rich sibling, being a successful influencer with her own house while Nora works for a dying record label and is forced to crash on Hannah's couch. Shipper on Deck: She tries to help Moog win over her sister.

characters.name: JJ
characters.description: The former bagel boy at Waxtown and Nora's ex-boyfriend, he's now a successful app developer. Ambiguously Evil: Played with and ultimately averted. He’s a constant source of annoyance for Nora, and she and Animal both think he’s bad news. He intends to fold Waxtown Records into his company, Jfinity, which is used to frame him as something of aCorrupt Corporate Executive, but he pulls strings more than once to help the band and eventually becomes a firm ally to them. Better as Friends:He clearly wants more, but by the end accepts that this'll be his relationship with Nora. The Charmer: Constantly buttering up Penny. Jerk with a Heart of Gold: He's not a bad guy at heart, but he took Nora's dumping him for not being ambitious to mean he had to become a schmoozing, smug exec to win her back.
